message:
feat(ops,tui): migrate to FrankenTUI backend and refine ops dashboard rendering

Replace ratatui/crossterm dependencies with the FrankenTUI (ftui-*) crate
family across the workspace, frankensearch-ops, and frankensearch-tui.

Ops TUI dashboard improvements:
- Thread SemanticPalette through all screen render paths and overlays so
  colors respond to theme/preference changes consistently
- Add per-screen KeybindingHint arrays (alerts, fleet, timeline, etc.)
  surfaced in the help overlay and status bar contextual hints
- Introduce compact rendering mode for small terminals (<120x16) across
  alerts/SLO, fleet, timeline, index-resources, and live-stream screens
- Add sparkline-based pulse indicators for alert severity/recency and
  SLO burn/latency pressure in the alerts screen
- Add incident badge, severity filter pills, and alert density sparkline
  to the alerts/SLO summary header
- Convert overlay rendering (help, alert, confirm, palette) to use the
  semantic palette for border, accent, and text styling
- Migrate all crossterm key/modifier references to ftui_core::event types
  (KeyCode, Modifiers, Escape vs Esc rename)

Core TUI framework improvements:
- Replace ratatui Frame/Layout/Style/Widget imports with ftui-render,
  ftui-layout, ftui-style, ftui-text, and ftui-widgets equivalents
- Migrate Layout::default().direction().constraints().split() pattern to
  Flex::vertical()/horizontal().constraints().split()
- Replace Constraint::Length with Constraint::Fixed, Constraint::Percentage
  now takes f32 instead of u16
- Switch from frame.area() to frame.bounds(), frame.render_widget(w, r)
  to w.render(r, frame)
- Add KeybindingHint struct and Screen::keybindings() default method for
  contextual help integration
- Build help overlay requests with merged global + screen-local hints
- Update shell status bar to show up to 2 contextual screen keybindings
  when terminal width >= 110
- Replace crossterm::terminal::size() fallback with static 80x24 default
- Migrate TtyBackend initialization using TtySessionOptions with
  alternate_screen and mouse_capture feature flags

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
